div.head: Memorandum by the Chief of the Division of Western European Affairs (Moffat)
div.p: With reference to the Swiss Chargé d’Affaires’s call of August 22, I ascertained that the position of this Government would be that our treaty relations with Cuba were on a special footing specifically recognized in all our unconditional most favored nation treaties. Accordingly, no third country would benefit by reductions granted to Cuba.
I also ascertained that the Department of Agriculture had not yet completed its regulations governing the admission of foodstuffs from abroad as a result of the drought and attendant speculation.
I informed Mr. Micheli on both these questions Thursday, August 23.
